scope: local-k3d only; no RunPod, cloud-dev, deploy/release, or broad UI redesign

task_1_builder_registry.regression_cause: AMOF_CLUSTER_BUILDER_IMAGE was rendered to host.k3d.internal:5000/amof-cluster-builder:<tag>, which k3d nodes cannot resolve via in-cluster DNS. Live deployment env confirmed host.k3d.internal:5000/amof-cluster-builder:up8-wave7-builder-buildkit-host-20260425224728.
task_1_builder_registry.fix_applied: Switched all *-local.yaml repository values from host.k3d.internal:5000/* to k3d-amof-registry:5000/* (cluster-builder, controlplane, dashboard, agent, registry.url). Added k3d-amof-registry:5000 to cluster-builder-docker-wrapper.sh buildkitd insecure registry list. Live AMOF_CLUSTER_BUILDER_IMAGE env updated via kubectl set env on deploy/amof-amof-control-plane-controlplane.
task_1_builder_registry.in_cluster_pull_proof: docker exec k3d-amof-server-0 crictl pull k3d-amof-registry:5000/amof-cluster-builder:up8-wave7-builder-buildkit-host-20260425224728 -> Image is up to date for sha256:675221cbe37e0c9a60a5f26442a05bfe0f8ef234728253a7b91dd236df8fd1f5

task_2_auth_badge_truth.regression_cause: repos/amof-ui/src/components/app-header.tsx fell through to the literal string 'Authenticated' whenever user?.email was undefined, including when the local lab runs auth-disabled (apiSession.auth_enabled=false, authenticated=false, can_access_console=true). Header therefore falsely implied a real session.
task_2_auth_badge_truth.fix_applied: When apiSession.auth_enabled === false, identityLabel now reads 'Auth disabled (local lab)' instead of 'Authenticated'. Behavior unchanged: console access remains permitted because /api/v1/auth/session still reports can_access_console=true. Only the wording was corrected.

live_proof.header_innertext_at_1600w: amof-platform / main / dev / Ecosystems / Auth disabled (local lab) / AMOF 1.5.0-rc.2 beta.2 / Supabase Media / amof.dev / Sign Out
